definitions.text: Every individual or anything of the given class, with no exceptions (the noun or noun phrase denoting the class must be plural or uncountable).
examples: All contestants must register for the footrace: we've arranged numbers for them all.
List all books of which you were the sole or co-author.

definitions.text: Throughout the whole of (a stated period of time; generally used with units of a day or longer).
examples: The store is open all day and all night.
I’ve been working on this all year.
He's all talk; he never puts his ideas into practice.
She knows all and sees all.
She was sitting all alone. It suddenly went all quiet.I'm all done, Mommy! I did it all by myself.
The score was 30 all when the rain delay started.

definitions.text: So much; used with "the" and a comparative.
examples: Don't want to go? All the better since I lost the tickets.
If he leave the company, I'll have to work all the harder.

definitions.text: Everything that one is capable of.
examples: She gave her all, and collapsed at the finish line.

definitions.text: Everything in general; all that matters.
examples: I will die fighting for my people, because my people are all.
